scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT COURT / CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT RELATING TO IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell in respect of the residential property bearing Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft (hereinafter referred to as the "Schedule Property"). The total sale consideration agreed upon between the parties was Rs. 85,00,000/-. A copy of the Agreement to Sell is marked as Annexure A.

6. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on the date of the agreement, i.e., 10.01.2024. The said payment was made via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. A copy of the bank receipt evidencing the said payment is marked as Annexure B.

7. As per the covenants contained in the Agreement to Sell, the balance sale consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024, against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff.

8. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the obligations under the Agreement to Sell. The Plaintiff arranged for the balance funds and remained prepared to pay the same upon the Defendant offering the proper conveyance within the stipulated time frame.

BREACH OF CONTRACT AND REFUSAL

9. On 05.06.2024, prior to the deadline for performance, the Defendant issued a letter addressed to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the Sale Deed. In the said communication, the Defendant stated that he intended to sell the Schedule Property to a third party as he had received a higher offer. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is marked as Annexure D.

10. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to honour the Agreement to Sell and execute the Sale Deed. The Defendant received the said notice but failed to comply with the requisition therein and maintained his refusal to perform the contract. A copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt is marked as Annexure C.

DAMAGES

11. By reason of the Defendant's wrongful refusal and failure to execute the Sale Deed, the Plaintiff has been deprived of her rightful interest in the Schedule Property and has suffered loss and inconvenience. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation for the breach of contract and for the delay in performance. The Plaintiff claims compensation quantified at Rs. {{COMPENSATION_AMOUNT}}/- towards the loss suffered due to the Defendant's breach, in addition to the relief of specific performance.

LEGAL BASIS

12. The present cause is governed by the Specific Relief Act, 1963, including Section 10 (mandatory after 2018 Amendment — court SHALL grant unless S.11(2), S.14, or S.16 exception), Section 16, Section 21, and Section 22.

13. The present cause is governed by the Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 10.

14. The Plaintiff also relies on the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, including Section 53A, where the pleaded facts attract those provisions.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

16. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

17. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

19. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024, directing the Defendant to execute and register the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
    (b) In the alternative, if specific performance is not granted, pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{COMPENSATION_AMOUNT}}/- towards damages for breach of contract;
    (c) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 19 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{A

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Commercial Court (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT Bengaluru

Commercial Suit No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A Company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A Limited Liability Partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act.

2. The suit is valued above the specified value of Rs. 32,00,000/- as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Court.

JURISDICTION

3. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

4. This Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

5. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when contract is broken; or for continuing breach, when it ceases. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

7. The Plaintiff has complied with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 regarding pre-institution mediation. {{MEDIATION_CERTIFICATE_DETAILS -- Insert Mediation Date, Authority, and Certificate Number if available, or claim exemption if urgent interim relief is sought}}.

FACTS OF THE CASE

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system for a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. (Annexure A)

9. In terms of the said Agreement, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment on 01.02.2024 via NEFT bearing UTR No. ICIC20240201456789. (Annexure B)

10. The Defendant was contractually obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

11. The Defendant failed to deliver any module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without returning the advance payment, as communicated via email correspondence. (Annexure C)

13. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant on 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to remedy the breach. (Annexure E)

BREACH PARTICULARS

14. The Defendant committed a breach of Clause {{AGREEMENT_CLAUSE_NUMBER}} of the Agreement by failing to deliver the first module by 01.05.2024.

15. The Defendant further breached the Agreement by abandoning the project on 15.06.2024 and retaining the advance payment without justification.

16. The Defendant has failed to comply with the requisitions made in the legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

DAMAGES

17. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the actual loss suffered comprising the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/-.

18. The Plaintiff suffered consequential damages quantified at Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by non-delivery, as assessed by the auditor. (Annexure D)

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest at 18% per annum on the outstanding amount from the date of breach until realization.

20. The total damages claimed amount to Rs. 32,00,000/-, being the aggregate of the refund and consequential damages, besides interest and costs.

LEGAL BASIS

21. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

22. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

23. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

24. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

25. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

26. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

27. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) towards damages;
    (b) Award Rs. 20,00,000/- towards Actual Loss;
    (c) Award Rs. 12,00,000/- towards Consequential loss / damages;
    (d) Award Rs. {{LIQUIDATED_DAMAGES_S74_AMOUNT}}/- towards Liquidated Damages S74;
    (e) Award Rs. {{INTEREST_ON_DELAYED_PAYMENT_AMOUNT}}/- towards Interest On Delayed Payment;
    (f) Award Rs. {{MITIGATION_CREDIT_AMOUNT}}/- towards Mitigation Credit;
    (g) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (h) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (i) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

28. Actual Loss
   Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
   Basis / methodology: Refund of advance payment
   Documentary proof: Annexure B (NEFT Receipt)

29. Consequential loss / damages
   Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
   Basis / methodology: Loss assessment report by auditor
   Documentary proof: Annexure D

30. Liquidated Damages S74
   Amount claimed: Rs. {{LIQUIDATED_DAMAGES_S74_AMOUNT}}/-
   Basis / methodology: {{LIQUIDATED_DAMAGES_S74_BASIS}}
   Documentary proof: {{LIQUIDATED_DAMAGES_S74_PROOF}}

31. Interest On Delayed Payment
   Amount claimed: Rs. {{INTEREST_ON_DELAYED_PAYMENT_AMOUNT}}/-
   Basis / methodology
